# Wednesday, July 2, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1948, Professor [Ralphael O'Hara Lanier](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFugzAQgOGnMRvobEyIBw9d2NK1I7rkDrB6sZHthOTtq_YV-gL_L31fl_mCQUJcP_HOvr6WNEc-inCtnGcUyUz5EWOIa3mUnW-Vqezv2YAZWhhbMDNhkPdsG_JXIKcb9noctB0suKHZPGk6D8Y63WtNzp207R27HhyMS3-GsQn-NwYjGK1B930HdmHUiyNifaJhUBaYQk25qxtXfmFZ5J2enLtbujfit1r3ovoPZSZlpuM4ulo2TFFC5C7lVZlpw0jXlL6VmTjWHLgoMwnGwLnNKPuGLG3aMGOTPe1HEAnlNCoL6x2D_I3q_3ie3vwEAAD__yKZeq8) became the first president of what is now Texas Southern University, which was established by the Texas Legislature in 1947 as an institution for black students. A new state law allows Texans to use gold and silver as legal tender, but there are a few twists. Check out the details in today's _Roundup_ section, and let us know what you think of [this development](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L9uwyAQgPGnwVui4wDbDAxZ3CmdKrUbwubsIBHbgsu_t6_SV-j6Db_h-z77c0g5rctnuJLj57z5lR41EzMVH3IuFMttXdO61FvdaWKKdX95BDQH6A6APoaUX1430Y0QrWzIyc5IbTRY01xcZyyOKkAbjcJRTtTLbpKtHo1q-15Sk9wbgw5QSpBKHUHPFORsYyTZRmOEBoqJt3LkCzE9Q53za7tTOU7btcnuwrxXoU4CB4HD810FDh-FltM4bsz-60fgUDnwrQocpFVWWdAGDFqlurbXTXFxf6ScU207oWG5hpT_dP7fk7vD3wAAAP__v3hzFg) by shooting us a reply. We think of _The Texas Flyover_ as more than a news publication—it's a family. And if you're reading this, you're a part of it. Every morning, we show up in your inbox hoping to offer a grounded, honest start to the day. And we are grateful that you start your day with us. This week, one of our own—David, our editor in chief and the second-longest serving member of _The Texas Flyover_ team—underwent surgery. He’s currently in the hospital recovering. We’d love to welcome him back with a wave of encouragement from the people he serves every day: _you._ If you’d like to send David a get-well note, just reply to this email. We’ll make sure he sees every message. Let’s fill his inbox with support from across _The Texas Flyover_ family. We're sponsored today by a _Flyover_ favorite, the [Sonic Glow Toothbrush](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0MFq4zAQgOGnkW8xMyNZjg86LAQvLJtdWgrtTcjWOBFVbCMpcfL2JX2F3n--w_9-tEcXYphP_9yFTblPi515y5FL4WRdjIl9us5zmE_5mlceC_u8PiwBNTtod0DWuxAfVlXeDOA7rNhg26BqFHRNdTa6VQ4mtW9RS-1ID6QGL92oJpBawlgF88SgBUIElLIGNbHDqfOeUfumEQrYh7Kkupy58N3lKT6WG6d6XC5VNOdS1izkL0G9oH7btnpL42eYhxifhaCefuMe8YW0oP7P31fEw_4Zy35M7Eq4sQ1eyEOjW9kJ0vk6oJCHt4_-f5WMX7cQY8i6FQpOFxe-1ar87NbN0FcAAAD__3H1exA).

## Allred Announces Senate Run
Former U.S. Rep. Colin Allred has announced he is [running for the U.S. Senate](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SozAQhJ8GMlHS8HcECi4h86UXUmPNANoSiJIGY-_Tb9mvsHFXd_X3_b9NN_TB78s_3NjKc47TzlcOLMJpwhASUzr33e9LPvPBTpjy8ZpAQ6t0rzRMhD68pqYke9c0mJKt6VvTtI0e2nK1bUedxmGumZHRDcBNNyMQ3YfeQT2U3r7HdK_BGG3qutLNzGjmgYhNR21bNJrJS0yVrCz8xDyHV3xwqlzcymBXkSMX9d8CxgLG67qqOT7fFO-8gPGIwYt3uYDx01bEW3QJRV1rVCFmUcKkXDq_1R-1xV3WrHCJ6uvcjqxAQ6cy7yisEjouk6Xj8iH43PVFo5cNffhckd8JfFj4CQAA__9l8oii) in a bid to become the first Democrat to win a statewide election in Texas since 1994. Last year, he lost a challenge to U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz by nearly nine percentage points despite Democrats spending more than $130 million on the race. Allred met with other prominent Democrats in May to discuss who would be the best to run for the seat currently held by Republican John Cornyn, who faces a tough primary contest with Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. _" Everyone else has to make their own decisions,"_ [said Allred](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGunDAQRb_GdLOyDQZt4WIblCIvqaKUaLCHxdFgI9v7WP4-IvmE113pSlf3nN8f0wcGDvH5Azey9b2kKdJRmGqlPCFzJp9fMYb4LK-yk6vky35OWmoDcgCpJ4-Bz6lrvJ2lv6uGrBqM6kwn76ZZbTt0fas6koOju5uVaeehV3JeeiN7o0wT7DUmB6mVkqptb7JbCNVy955U740RnSQfasq3ulKlN5aFz_RJ-ebS1rBda92LaB9Cj0KPx3HcthKvTuiRIryK0OMFJfS4Jw41uCt6ZMYCnrbkMlZwiUOE_8TA-IpupQKFIlYCh9uO4RlhSRkKYYWV2MN8wp-0RnApxzMKPWKGx0N9-_6r_9lk6_cjMIfSD6KTzw0D_3tcv-b50-q_AQAA___U85Rj), who joins retired astronaut Terry Virts in seeking the Democratic nomination. Allred said his campaign will aim to provide solutions to the rising cost of living and expose the flaws of "corrupt" politicians. _" I know Washington is broken. The system is rigged, but it doesn't have to be this way,"_ [he said](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAURp8Gb0QXDHE9MHTxlq4drYu52FQYLCB_b18lfYTOn3Q-nfN9mS8YYkjrF-5k2sPnOdG9RmqNyowxFnLlmlJIa73Wg5ZGrh7PWYLUHAYOcnYY4nNWnTMW3Cg6MmLQQmkFo-42Y0dre5TOKkVaWfRWePAanAf0i8UumBcMBpBCgOj7EyhPKPzoHImz05opIBdaLqe2UaMHVh-f-UbltOS9i2Zr7ais_2RyYnJqG20hxtfG5LTlnV5CTE4L7geGNTE56X74UOPAlxxD4n-W_CeHVPmbzyslbMQLLsTk1BXjjnuIMdTzwBSsO4b3Qdf-l-xm5G8AAAD__9BCgrs). Allred outperformed Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris in Texas during the 2024 election, and a [recent poll](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1ywyAQhJ9G6qQB9BcKijTqnDal5qQ7rJtgYADZ1ttn7DxC-t1vZ7_vy3IBduyvX3AjU542LJ4e2VEplBZwLhGmw3v213zkSFshzPFclFBDI6ZGqAWB3bn0NZpVoJY1GTkNsh96oYd6N2iVBr0J2izhKOyKk7bDqDR8TKNWXc3mBROTUFIK2XWt6C2BtBqR5IjDUPWCkEtIbdmp0BOydWe4U2q3cKud2UuJueo-KzVXan4H4rE63kJkz8EnygRp29t8rLnA9vPqVWqOlZot3EOClR2Xs0lQXj8b8NhAk-iPAr5OBuODneM8TlUvrjdg9x4v_1N2N-o3AAD__511hO4) shows he enjoys high favorability ratings in the state. The midterm elections are likely to be challenging for Republicans, whose candidate for U.S. Senate in Texas could struggle to coalesce party support after a contentious primary.
## Local Campaign Updates
While news of Colin Allred’s campaign for the U.S. Senate has dominated headlines, there have also been significant recent developments in two state senate races and the election to succeed the late Democratic Rep. Sylvester Turner of Houston. State Sen. Brian Birdwell (R-Granbury) said Monday he would [not seek reelection](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGSrCAUAE-DGRagSBkQ_MRsfrqhhfBUahGsx3Ocuf3W7BX2At1d_fWYHy6mmLf_7gBLr7XMGe6agAhwdikhBLxyjnmrVz3BE4R6vmcllObCcKHm4GJ6z30T7CLCKBuw0mjZ616Mutmt0V6swSgjpfKjdr6TfoEhOC8kBKOaaD8wYYSSUsiua0W_gpPrGALIIWjNegEhUsGWdiB4ubqmd3kCtr4cTbI70VlZ94-pialpLUh3QdoRzoLUFtyYmj4KpiYxMDV1gqnJVb5EDDekxM_kcuUIFBEOyMRXLAevkB0B96V88-Su7Heo3LvjdHHLnApfgO-x8np5D7UWZGpq0IbzjinFOhjWi-1wMf1m0t_mPq36CQAA__9YrJFC) after representing parts of North and Central Texas since 2010. State Rep. David Cook (R-Mansfield), who lost a contest for speaker of the Texas House to State Rep. Dustin Burrows (R-Lubbock), indicated he would run for the vacant seat shortly after Birdwell’s announcement. In Tarrant County, State Rep. Nate Schatzline (R-Fort Worth) [dropped out of the race](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SgyAQx58GOxxA0bOgSONck2uvdAi7RGYQHVhjkqe_ia9w7W93_l-_1-lqQwzp_mMXNPT065TwKBGJME82xoyQ95RCupe9bOgIoWyvSQmluei5UBPYEF9TW4G5CRhkhUb2Wra6FYOuZuOHRrSDbpTzKHvopJIddLb5ulm4KcQqmI-Y6IWSUsimqUXr0Uo_AKDsQGvWCoRAa65pRsKnLT6-1gfm2q1LFc1MtBXWXJgamRqP46iXkj43pkZMfC9MjZ9STI3bGgMFdxJLyIubLb1jSMjxGajwbB1yv2Z--vCC55v1hJknPLizCQKcLKV1Tw4LnzHzvCemRpv55SK_3yLqKhvYjhBjKF3PWnFfbIhnYPrfzA-j_gIAAP__lBSVUw) to succeed former State Sen. Kelly Hancock (R-North Richland Hills) after local activist Leigh Wambsganss (R-Southlake) announced her intent to run. Wambsganss was quickly endorsed by Schatzline and Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick. Finally, legendary boxer George Foreman’s son, [George Foreman IV](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO6yAUAE-DO1uPZ7C_C4rfuMu2W1oEHjYSBgQkTm6_yl5h65FmpPm-bTftg4_7lz5JtZdLW6SrBmqNyqZDKGTLI0Yf9_qomUwjW_N7Q0DZw9wDblb78N5EZ9Ud7MI7UnyWXEgBi-wOhWgmGOVyl8s0jQtO_7hBK5y7L1Ijl51XHxnMgJwDH8cBhCPN3WIt8clKyQSQ9S2VoR3U6KWrC-_0pDKYdHZBHa3lysb_DFeG63VdgzlKih_KcM0p-OZNZbjq0rwJxHDdKZWdepcKnTr2_tmbFPdCtfYIAlAADPnIXVE2Xz4EX6eZCdhP7cNvtf3t1VPhTwAAAP__UR1-Tw), announced he is running for the 18th Congressional District in Houston, which has been vacant since former Houston mayor Sylvester Turner died in March. Foreman will run as an independent and joins a field of 29 candidates.
## Gold, Silver Designated Legal Tender in Texas
A new state law [designates gold and silver as legal tender](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD3S2yAQhk8jdXgACysqKNKoc9qUmrV2QUxWwACW7dtn5Ct8_fv7_L0vdwgcov8DO9n2dmmJ9KpMrVFZgLkQlmeMIfr6rJnWRljzZ9FSGyFHIfWCEPizDD3ah8RJ9WTVaNRgBjmZfrPa3HB9_BpHpbQBUhK1hIdWQKgmN7g-2DNMjlIrJdX1epGDI1BuQiR1Q2O6QRKGlsqlbdToDdXxJx1ULmvae7Zba7l219-dnjs9rynERky-QN5ORafn81Kn569X7PCPqvCJUdTABxXB5IFFo4hUhEvM6RWiF_7siKmIKiDnkg7gvljMr8Ac6m3sBul3CPyd0X4G77D6fwAAAP__FieH_Q) in Texas and outlines a process for conducting transactions. However, it stops short of requiring retailers to accept the precious metals as payment, and it doesn’t prohibit the use of U.S. currency. House Bill 1056 by State Rep. Mark Dorazio (R-San Antonio) aims to provide a framework for the clause in Article I, Section 10 of the U.S. Constitution, that stipulates that no state shall “make any thing but gold and silver coin a tender in payment of debts.” The [law requires](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuupDAQAE9jskHtxoYhcLAbWJvMBptsiAxuwFKDEe753f7pzRVeXqqS6v9tuIXEaV_-ho2cvOY87PQsTCJ0DoH5pHje9z3tS7mXgyahWI73gID2At0FcIgh8XswVXQjxF5X5HRntbEGelutriXTzAZiTwAUeovXaHXQONKIsZupSu5bBh2g1qCbpgYzU9BzHyPpNlqrDFBMks9aVhJ6hTLzOz_orKe8VexWkaOo5pdCr9BP4UiSuf6A9ZIfCr1wjnkqCv21_6fQj4lZ6CUK_SobK_R_foMG2_p6la06XTyeiTmVtlMGli0k_rTkZ4ceDr8CAAD___hLerA) that gold or silver must be “marked with the specie's weight and purity” to be used as legal tender, and it allows the comptroller to establish an electronic payment system for precious metals held in the Texas Bullion Depository. Banking associations opposed the legislation, claiming the framework lacks consumer protections, while other critics suggest that paying with precious metals could incur capital gains taxes. Proponents argue that gold and silver provide a [hedge against inflation](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2u4yAUxfHV4M4Wn2ZcUEzjLtNOaRG42Eg3gIDE8e6fki28-ki_I_3_37abjRjT_s8-wPR3yFuCsyH0DnWziBV8faYU096erYDr4Fu5Nk65GqkeKd-8jXhtcvDmTv3CBjBMKyaVpIsaDjO7WQfLYJFcCyXvdpbyjwiaeSHmwPUQzQejmnLGKBNiojKAZWHxHtjslSKSgo8916kf0OFtW8Arv6BOLj8GNEfvpRHxl_CV8PU8z8kdNafPSvhaMsYeXSN8tbVHh0D4umf0Y4v4gjp-xdE9a4XkrpFTSRljYipHGarx5YyIsc2aSLo_bMTva_9dq5fhPwEAAP__oPl-qw) and government manipulation of fiat currency.

Ted Cruz by nearly 9 percentage points despite Democrats spending more than $130 million on the race. | | | | | | Wednesday, July 2, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1948, Professor became the first president of what is now Texas Southern University, which was established by the Texas Legislature in 1947 as an institution for black students. A new state law allows Texans to use gold and silver as legal tender, but there are a few twists. Check out the details in today's Roundup section, and let us know what you think of by shooting us a reply. We think of The Texas Flyover as more than a news publication—it's a family. And if you're reading this, you're a part of it. Every morning, we show up in your inbox hoping to offer a grounded, honest start to the day. And we are grateful that you start your day with us. This week, one of our own—David, our editor in chief and the second-longest serving member of The Texas Flyover team—underwent surgery. He’s currently in the hospital recovering. We’d love to welcome him back with a wave of encouragement from the people he serves every day: you. If you’d like to send David a get-well note, just reply to this email. We’ll make sure he sees every message. Let’s fill his inbox with support from across The Texas Flyover family. We're sponsored today by a Flyover favorite, the . Tune in to The Flyover Podcast! New episodes drop daily—watch on YouTube or listen on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, all in under 15 minutes. | | Allred Announces Senate Run Former U.S. Rep. Colin Allred has announced he is in a bid to become the first Democrat to win a statewide election in Texas since 1994. Last year, he lost a challenge to U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz by nearly nine percentage points despite Democrats spending more than $130 million on the race. Allred met with other prominent Democrats in May to discuss who would be the best to run for the seat currently held by Republican John Cornyn, who faces a tough primary contest with Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. " Everyone else has to make their own decisions," , who joins retired astronaut Terry Virts in seeking the Democratic nomination. Allred said his campaign will aim to provide solutions to the rising cost of living and expose the flaws of "corrupt" politicians. " I know Washington is broken. The system is rigged, but it doesn't have to be this way," . Allred outperformed Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris in Texas during the 2024 election, and a shows he enjoys high favorability ratings in the state. The midterm elections are likely to be challenging for Republicans, whose candidate for U.S. Senate in Texas could struggle to coalesce party support after a contentious primary. Local Campaign Updates While news of Colin Allred’s campaign for the U.S. Senate has dominated headlines, there have also been significant recent developments in two state senate races and the election to succeed the late Democratic Rep. Sylvester Turner of Houston. State Sen. Brian Birdwell (R-Granbury) said Monday he would after representing parts of North and Central Texas since 2010. State Rep. David Cook (R-Mansfield), who lost a contest for speaker of the Texas House to State Rep. Dustin Burrows (R-Lubbock), indicated he would run for the vacant seat shortly after Birdwell’s announcement. In Tarrant County, State Rep. Nate Schatzline (R-Fort Worth) to succeed former State Sen. Kelly Hancock (R-North Richland Hills) after local activist Leigh Wambsganss (R-Southlake) announced her intent to run. Wambsganss was quickly endorsed by Schatzline and Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick. Finally, legendary boxer George Foreman’s son, , announced he is running for the 18th Congressional District in Houston, which has been vacant since former Houston mayor Sylvester Turner died in March. Foreman will run as an independent and joins a field of 29 candidates. Gold, Silver Designated Legal Tender in Texas A new state law in Texas and outlines a process for conducting transactions. However, it stops short of requiring retailers to accept the precious metals as payment, and it doesn’t prohibit the use of U.S. currency. House Bill 1056 by State Rep. Mark Dorazio (R-San Antonio) aims to provide a framework for the clause in Article I, Section 10 of the U.S. Constitution, that stipulates that no state shall “make any thing but gold and silver coin a tender in payment of debts.” The that gold or silver must be “marked with the specie's weight and purity” to be used as legal tender, and it allows the comptroller to establish an electronic payment system for precious metals held in the Texas Bullion Depository. Banking associations opposed the legislation, claiming the framework lacks consumer protections, while other critics suggest that paying with precious metals could incur capital gains taxes. Proponents argue that gold and silver provide a and government manipulation of fiat currency. | Flying together with our sponsor Celebrate a brighter smile this 4th of July with the —now on special holiday offer! name: America First War Room Sponsor
subject: Bitcoin Was the Prototype. NatGold Is the Masterpiece.
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
[View in Web Browser](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8kMFu6yAQRb8GdrFgwEAWLN5r5FayarVqo6rZIcANlYkdcOz476ukabYzZ-YeXROHLrSLRvI_AtgHlxEAYv8QwPFzjun0HevH1Xmp69VRNLG8bOHhhk5whw_WyGX3JppmmzLfpaf5-XU30Ts-LoO_w7fQwkcTustUbrDTrHWEY6-pLCkvSw4C7zXzngsp1msrvGoNKMUYdxQ8a1uijMBBA4GSSAKUAlBWWA6K-lbZ0om18xRxcs0pTPQpWEPzOJuU-j4Wto-40_txHPJVrEJQzakvDifb-VP-PbN9RFDdlBFUE4LqPMj3OCjhGsSqa2lsY16aZluL4wdO2g1z6LqQhUScfP29wZOGnwAAAP__nRJvAw)
| |
---
| | | Bitcoin changed everything. It proved the world was ready to opt out of fiat — to build wealth outside the reach of governments and banks. But it also had major flaws:
* It's backed by nothing but faith
* It's wildly volatile
* And it burns more electricity than Argentina

That was the prototype.

---|---|---
Photo by [Shahadat Rahman](https://substack.com/redirect/f5ef7bb6-6b5f-41fb-87be-afb3a71f4e16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/07fcf448-7752-4b61-9852-0bc466c0c842?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Kratom, derived from the leaves of the Mitragyna speciosa tree native to Southeast Asia, is an herbal supplement that has sparked significant debate due to its complex effects. The plant's primary alkaloids, mitragynine and 7-hydroxymitragynine, interact with opioid receptors in the brain, producing effects ranging from pain relief and relaxation at higher doses to stimulation and increased energy at lower doses. Kratom is commonly consumed as a powder, capsule, or tea and is sold in gas stations, vape shops, and online. Advocates praise its potential for managing chronic pain, anxiety, and opioid withdrawal, with an estimated 15 million Americans, including 100,000 Mississippians, using it for these purposes.
However, critics, including medical professionals, warn of its addictive potential and risks, likening it to opioids due to its effects on the brain. The FDA classifies kratom as a dietary supplement, not a controlled substance, but has raised concerns about its safety, citing cases of overdose and dependency.
In Mississippi, the debate over kratom's regulation culminated in the passage of House Bill 1077, signed into law by Governor Tate Reeves on April 24, 2025, and effective July 1, 2025. Authored by Representative Lee Yancey (R-Brandon), the Kratom Consumer Protection Act aims to balance access to kratom with public safety concerns. The bill passed unanimously in both the House (115-0) and Senate (51-0), reflecting broad legislative support. HB 1077 introduces two key regulations.
First, it restricts kratom purchases to individuals aged 21 and older, imposing fines of up to $1,000 and misdemeanor charges for repeat violations within five years for both underage buyers and retailers who sell to them. Retailers must verify age, place kratom products behind counters, and ensure employees sign agreements acknowledging the age restriction.
Second, the law bans synthetic kratom extracts, specifically those with high concentrations of 7-hydroxymitragynine (exceeding 1% of the alkaloid composition or 0.5 mg per container), due to their heightened potency and addictive potential compared to "pure" kratom leaf products.
The legislation responds to growing concerns about kratom's accessibility, particularly to youth, and its presence in unregulated forms. Over 30 Mississippi counties and cities had already imposed local bans or restrictions, reflecting unease about its availability at gas stations. Medical experts, including Dr. Jennifer Bryan of the Mississippi Medical Association, have labeled kratom "the next phase of the opioid crisis," citing its addictive properties.
However, advocates like Christina Dent of End It For Good argue that regulation, rather than criminalization, protects users by ensuring access to safer, pure kratom while preventing a dangerous black market. HB 1077 was one of four kratom-related bills proposed in 2025; others, which sought to classify kratom as a Schedule III controlled substance, failed, indicating a legislative preference for regulation over outright bans.
While HB 1077 does not supersede local bans, it sets a statewide standard, preserving the right of counties and cities to enforce stricter rules. For users, the law ensures continued access to kratom for pain relief or opioid cessation, but for retailers, it mandates compliance with new age and product restrictions, potentially reshaping the market. The law's impact will depend on enforcement and whether it curbs misuse while maintaining access for responsible users.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 7/2/2025
body:
How rural Colorado is building the energy future the state won’t
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi friends,
_We’ve been having some issues with email delivery lately. If this landed in your inbox, we’d be so grateful if you opened, clicked, and forwarded it to a friend. Every bit of engagement helps us reach more people with the truth._
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Colorado’s wolf reintroduction has [cost taxpayers over $3 million](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—quadruple projections—and CPW is quietly pushing permanent wolf protections through a new ballot measure. A new study finds 70% of land zoned for housing [bans affordable options](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) like duplexes and townhomes.
Rural communities are [building their own energy solutions](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) as state mandates fall short, while Grand Junction faces scrutiny for quietly planning an $8.5M pedestrian bridge amid an [eminent domain dispute](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). And Boulder mourns the loss of Jewish leader Karen Diamond, [killed in a firebomb attack](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) that injured 28 at a pro-Israel rally.
**🇺🇸 On the National Stage...** Trump’s “Big, Beautiful Bill” [passed the Senate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) with VP Vance’s tie-breaking vote, reshaping immigration and energy policy. Biden’s DOE is under fire for [rushing $42 billion in green loans](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). ICE officials warn a [CNN-promoted app](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) could endanger federal agents.
**🗣️ Commentary Spotlight...** Cory Gaines warns CPW is being hijacked to [force a fur ban voters rejected](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Pastor Bob rebukes a military officer for [defying Trump’s transgender policy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) order, calling it a threat to discipline and biblical principles.
This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, July 2, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[“A deadline without a plan”: how rural Colorado is building the energy future the state won’t](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
In May 2023, Gov. Jared Polis signed HB 23‐1247, directing the Colorado Energy Office to study advanced energy solutions — from nuclear and geothermal to long-duration storage — in regions facing coal-plant closures like Craig Station. The law included $50,000 from the Just Transition Fund and federal support to study firm energy options in northwest Colorado.
Within months, coal facilities began closing across the state—including Craig Station, now set to shutter by 2028. While studies are underway, no comprehensive transition plan exists. Facing job losses and shrinking tax bases, rural communities are taking charge.
“There’s a closing schedule—but no roadmap,” said Matt Solomon, project manager for the Northwest Colorado Energy Initiative. “Colorado has a date and not a plan... What does that do? It erodes our trust in each other and our communities.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Gaines: Activists are using CPW to sneak in what the public rejected](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Despite Denver voters rejecting a fur ban last November, animal rights activists are now attempting to push the same policy statewide through a petition to Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW).
* The proposed rule would bypass public input and be decided by 12 unelected commissioners, all appointed by Governor Polis—raising serious concerns about democratic accountability.
* Commentary author Cory Gaines criticizes this end-run around voters as part of a larger trend of out-of-state interest groups manipulating Colorado’s unelected boards to impose unpopular policies.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
_**[Pastor Bob: Careful, Major—you serve at the pleasure of the President](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_ [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Rev. Babcox criticizes a U.S. Army Major who publicly opposed President Trump’s order banning transgender individuals from military service, arguing that lawful orders must be followed regardless of personal opinion.

* Drawing on his 21 years of Navy experience, Babcox contends that military service is too mentally and physically demanding for individuals struggling with gender dysphoria.
* He supports his argument with biblical references, claiming it is neither immoral nor unethical to exclude transgender individuals from the military, and warns the Major of the consequences of defying presidential authority.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️**

**[Union demands vs. rural realities: Safeway strike spreads to Fort Morgan](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN | COMMENTARY | JUNE 30** _Safeway employees in Fort Morgan are now part of an expanding labor walkout unfolding across Colorado, as union members demand greater transparency, long-term healthcare funding, and accountability from one of the nation’s largest grocery chains._

**[Devotional: When your worldview wears on you like sagging pants](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY DRAKE HUNTER | COMMENTARY | JUNE 29** _We are now into our series, The Belt of Truth. Additionally, we covered the various aspects we will explore throughout this series: subjective, objective, normative, absolute, and applied truth. Let’s dive into subjective truth to keep the series moving. Shall we?_ __

He believed July 2nd, not the 4th, would be remembered as “the most memorable epocha in the history of America.”

But if you provide a child with a rotten education, nothing happens, except that you're liable to be given more money to do it with. Well, we've discovered that money alone isn't the answer." – Ronald Reagan

A new study finds 70% of land zoned for housing like duplexes and townhomes. This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, July 2, 2025. – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY JEN SCHUMANN In May 2023, Gov. Jared Polis signed HB 23‐1247, directing the Colorado Energy Office to study advanced energy solutions — from nuclear and geothermal to long-duration storage — in regions facing coal-plant closures like Craig Station. The law included $50,000 from the Just Transition Fund and federal support to study firm energy options in northwest Colorado. Within months, coal facilities began closing across the state—including Craig Station, now set to shutter by 2028. While studies are underway, no comprehensive transition plan exists. Facing job losses and shrinking tax bases, rural communities are taking charge. “There’s a closing schedule—but no roadmap,” said Matt Solomon, project manager for the Northwest Colorado Energy Initiative. “Colorado has a date and not a plan... What does that do? It erodes our trust in each other and our communities.” | Despite Denver voters rejecting a fur ban last November, animal rights activists are now attempting to push the same policy statewide through a petition to Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW). The proposed rule would bypass public input and be decided by 12 unelected commissioners, all appointed by Governor Polis—raising serious concerns about democratic accountability. Commentary author Cory Gaines criticizes this end-run around voters as part of a larger trend of out-of-state interest groups manipulating Colorado’s unelected boards to impose unpopular policies. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. # [Tell the House to Kill Trump's Big Bill](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbf1948fb-e82b-4ad4-b646-c67f7aecfa00/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T37a105ca-aa7f-44a5-8168-df4f6f30a181/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | The Senate just passed Trump's "Big" bill in a 50-50 vote (with Vance as tie-breaker), and now this bucket of s*** (trillions in debt, millions losing health care, etc.) goes to the House for another vote. [**_Tell the House to kill it._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbdabb6f0-5e40-48fd-b05f-7a13f09629d7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Multiple House Republicans are currently on record against the Senate version, with self-professed "deficit hawks" particularly miffed that it ads more debt than any other bill in America's history. Will these Republicans cave? Probably. But this is far from a slam dunk. There's a razor-thin GOP majority, and some of them have already defied Trump in earlier votes. With enough pressure, we could stop this. And if it still passes, let it get as much attention as possible, so their raging hypocrisy and willingness to hurt Americans is abundantly clear to all, and fresh in the memories of voters come next election day. [_**Add your name: Tell the House to Kill Trump's Big Bill.**_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T88dd6d70-6201-4c28-9ce4-542ef72019c8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

That happens, but we are reminded that there is still a problem in Eastern Europe, specifically in Ukraine, where it's still [...] |

# [Big Beautiful Hypocrisy](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167182964&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTE0NTk2NzYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDA1MTY3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zz2YpUoFApy04l35PijBcZxVmXg-vzbFDLHYOTJF48c)
### Young people understand that the Republican Congressional bill borrows against our future. That creates an opportunity for Democrats.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Jul 2
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167182964&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MTQ1OTY3NiwiZXhwIjoxNzU0MDUxNjc2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lLRClFLRm3dWgSYPQHwdqaW9Cu5SLwg0hHNFsyaEq9k&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167182964&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTE0NTk2NzYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDA1MTY3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zz2YpUoFApy04l35PijBcZxVmXg-vzbFDLHYOTJF48c&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167182964&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTE0NTk2NzYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDA1MTY3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zz2YpUoFApy04l35PijBcZxVmXg-vzbFDLHYOTJF48c)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYmVuc2FtdWVscy9wL3JlcHVibGljYW4tYmlnLWJlYXV0aWZ1bC1oeXBvY3Jpc3k_.V1r4VWSX44pVAfbu6orOZU7B1Kq93tMeETYgGHsU3SQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/republican-big-beautiful-hypocrisy?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Congress is poised to pass Trump's signature legislation--the [One Big Beautiful Bill Act](https://substack.com/redirect/a2a7b4f3-9097-41f0-a471-c5c98d783fb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--at some point soon.1
I won't get into the nuance, because these sorts of budget bills are endlessly complex.2 But here's what I will say:
1. It's really bad for the country, especially for some of our neediest citizens. Even Republican Lisa Murkowski, who voted for the bill, [offered this understatement of the day](https://substack.com/redirect/60fd640e-ca98-41b6-bb4c-2db1f5c0ba5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): "I know that in many parts of the country, there are Americans that are not going to be advantaged by this bill."3
2. It balloons our debt, which means the interest on that debt will cost taxpayers a lot more. Over time, that will take money away from the things that the federal government can--and should--be spending money on.
3. Democrats have a unique opportunity to position themselves as the party of responsive, responsible, and restrained government.

[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzUxNDU5Njc2LCJleHAiOjIwNjcwMzU2NzYsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.V5o-WVPGJ8JlOUWEnSxdj-hzgHEdw5RQgrhOTwA6q6o?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _The government already spends nearly $900 billion per year paying interest on our debt. It 's about to go way up, to about $1.6 trillion by 2034._
> * _For a party that 's complained for years about America's ballooning federal debt, Republican hypocrisy on this bill is staggering._
> * _Younger voters believe that the federal government is wasteful, and young people are very concerned that they 'll never attain financial security. Democrats have a chance to win back young voters, if they can be responsive to that (reasonable) fear._
>

### Context on the "Big Beautiful Bill"
Before I get into anything related to how much it costs: the policies themselves are really bad. A few examples:
* Nonpartisan government estimates suggest that [11.8 million Americans will lose their health insurance](https://substack.com/redirect/f4afb659-e8b0-4b8d-9771-64c6e7e2b80e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) because of Medicaid cuts.
* Cuts to food stamps ([SNAP](https://substack.com/redirect/182f5e2c-0231-4321-8b95-9af93e749585?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) will impact many of the [40+ million Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/3e3c3693-b984-4ff3-ad2f-f1d5be9377e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who rely on the program.
* It drastically cuts support for clean energy in a way that [will increase energy costs](https://substack.com/redirect/f6575049-a3da-410a-9d36-8615b4fab4e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), to say nothing of the impact it'll have on the climate.

For a bill that's set to extend tax cuts for many Americans, it's [remarkable how unpopular it is](https://substack.com/redirect/fa25d564-a249-4c94-acff-b3ac3b4c558f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): **54% of Americans believe it 'll have a "mostly negative" impact on the country, and only 30% say its impact will be "mostly positive."**4
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167182964&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTE0NTk2NzYsImV4cCI6MTc1NDA1MTY3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zz2YpUoFApy04l35PijBcZxVmXg-vzbFDLHYOTJF48c)
### Context on how much our debt costs us
I'm not an economist, and I don't have any strong opinion on what the "right" levels of debt are for the country--lots of much smarter, much better educated people than I disagree on that question. **But as a statement of fact: the more the national debt grows, the more taxpayers have to spend on interest payments.** And we're about to add [$3,300,000,000,000 ($3.3 trillion) to our debt](https://substack.com/redirect/eb914b56-a85f-451d-aa09-8ddfda9bedc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), if not more.
In 2024--riding a wave of spending and borrowing coming out of the pandemic that hasn't abated--the American taxpayer spends more to pay down our debt than we do on Medicare.5
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4a73d2ca-a713-421a-b488-908a6d4ef9a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This bill takes things even further: it'll increase the federal government's annual interest payments from $870 billion to [$1.6 trillion by 2034](https://substack.com/redirect/74bfed13-8a73-4062-9700-e95662b655ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
It's almost impossible to contextualize how large that number is, so to give you some sense:
* The cost of [all the real estate in New York City is $1.48 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/f2684937-9991-4487-b0d4-08da05e34582?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* The total cost of all education in the U.S.--preschool through graduate school, public and private--[is about $1.4 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/ef84f491-cf3d-4683-8aa2-f6004224e172?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Those are both less than how much we'll be paying in interest _every year_.
People in Congress are constantly talking about the debt and the deficit, so **it 's important to note that this isn't normal**. For a century, debt levels have never been at 0%--there are [real benefits to the government borrowing money](https://substack.com/redirect/7f0ad1e8-1b54-441b-8e96-3c49c89806c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--but the more money that we borrow, the more money we have to spend paying off what we borrow. In that sense, it's not much different than a mortgage.
When borrowing is lower during more stable periods, it gives the country more flexibility to spend and borrow during moments of true crisis.
It made some sense to borrow money when the world briefly shut down during the COVID-19 pandemic, because that helped keep people fed and housed. But right now, our debt-to-GDP ratio is [higher than it was even during World War II](https://substack.com/redirect/5b0253ef-abdc-4f7f-9bdc-e1919b9534ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which is really concerning because we're hardly in a moment of crisis:
* The stock market is [at all-time highs](https://substack.com/redirect/504b3fe2-2acd-4694-b712-d5b65ac22d79?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg),6 having rebounded from the carnage earlier this year.
* Unemployment [is reasonably low](https://substack.com/redirect/42144e2a-fc18-4123-8717-057295b385a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Inflation has [been declining since 2023](https://substack.com/redirect/919a1b8b-4bd3-4c04-b04d-19b15b0d749c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), midway through the Biden Presidency.
* [GDP declined in the first few months](https://substack.com/redirect/71cd0834-2670-4806-ab62-7226eaf24473?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the Trump presidency, but only very slightly. It grew meaningfully from the pandemic through the end of 2024.

What does this mean? When a true crisis comes again--and it will, sooner or later--we're hamstringing our ability to spend money to curtail a deeper crisis, or to fund a war.
### Increasing the deficit for me, not for thee
Complaining about the other party's profligate, irresponsible spending is a time-tested Washington tradition.
But for a party that (nominally) cares about responsible spending, Republicans are awfully silent when they're in power. Here's a side-by-side comparison of what Republicans were saying during debates around Biden's [Inflation Reduction Act](https://substack.com/redirect/f716ae4d-c936-43c3-8277-2949908d0fbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) vs. what they're saying now when it comes to spending and/or the federal debt.7
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ebb267cd-a6e0-4631-bf37-b88c0e83f80e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This doesn't get talked about enough: the Congressional Budget Office said that Biden's Inflation Reduction Act _[reduced](https://substack.com/redirect/b522eaef-c664-45ef-9a42-d94e17e09edc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ the deficit by $238 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/b522eaef-c664-45ef-9a42-d94e17e09edc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Conservative think tanks [later questioned this math](https://substack.com/redirect/635fee29-fe6b-425a-a118-7ec131a6ec5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but you know what they weren't saying? That it was [increasing our debt by $3.3 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/6da8aea4-4c51-4078-a669-09ddafb6867f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Again, numbers that large are hard to contextualize (or at least they are for me),8 and--it's a nuanced but important distinction--debt and deficit are not the same thing. But: the fact that we are taking on _so_ much debt, and the fact that there's seemingly no Republican consideration for the long-term costs of what we're doing, is really bad.
They're not fooling the American public, by the way. In a recent poll, [3× more Americans believe](https://substack.com/redirect/fa25d564-a249-4c94-acff-b3ac3b4c558f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that this will increase the budget deficit than believe it'll reduce it.
### Democrats have an opportunity to win back young voters
I'm not an especially cynical person, or at least I try not to be.
But I've been paying into Social Security for as long as I've been working, and I've long assumed that I'll never see any benefit.9 And I'm not alone in this: 39% of millennials (and 45% of Gen Z) [believe that Social Security will have totally dried](https://substack.com/redirect/a7fce086-fa43-4d0c-a2ae-2efa3f4748a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) up by the time that we retire. Nearly half of people under 30 [believe that they won't be able to attain financial security](https://substack.com/redirect/eb1a7a11-bc62-4a86-b93d-4aadb4dfcc1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Anecdotally, I've felt--and [I'm](https://substack.com/redirect/501c3b5f-03de-4223-9d6b-205293e4f979?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [not](https://substack.com/redirect/0ee1ce4e-50c9-41e5-9c08-4090fbcdbfef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [alone](https://substack.com/redirect/81a98293-2a98-4378-a3bb-9a19834fe7cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [in](https://substack.com/redirect/0776b3c2-a0cf-4db0-89ef-1757bd33fca2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [having](https://substack.com/redirect/29f50911-f418-4969-ad45-ac3731dc61b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [noticed](https://substack.com/redirect/c13aa1d0-6ed4-42ea-8a9d-1caae8d70a7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [this](https://substack.com/redirect/e1d61412-11dc-4aaf-9870-c9810b540b3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--that Gen Z is an intensely cynical generation, in stark contrast to my more optimistic millennial peers. So it's notable that of Trump's signature ideas and policies, [the one that voters under 30 support most](https://substack.com/redirect/eb1a7a11-bc62-4a86-b93d-4aadb4dfcc1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE):
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/eb1a7a11-bc62-4a86-b93d-4aadb4dfcc1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I've written before about how [young men moved away from Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/dac770ca-67e6-48cb-8a32-5fb804cc6552?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the '24 election. But a dynamic that I haven't talked enough about: [young women did too, albeit not by the same clip](https://substack.com/redirect/8366e871-71b8-49c3-b974-633342d1e661?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).10
Perhaps their support for a better, more efficient government helps explain part of that trend. [DOGE has done extremely little to help Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/b7cd131f-3afa-4ab2-a0ca-1222f6083838?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but young people are right to believe [that government doesn't always spend taxpayer money efficiently](https://substack.com/redirect/1c51059e-bc2d-495f-ac27-2e847b1cb24d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The monstrosity of a bill that's likely to land on Trump's desk soon **creates an opportunity for Democrats to own the issue of government efficiency**. Republicans are spending with no regard for the future, and young voters--both men and women--understand that they're the ones who'll have to pay for it.
People want government to work better for them. But Republicans don't seem to care.
And while this isn't the only way to win back young voters, **demonstrating that Democrats actually care about our future --and that they're not just borrowing against it--is a start**.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY3MTgyOTY0LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzUxNDU5Njc2LCJleHAiOjIwNjcwMzU2NzYsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.V5o-WVPGJ8JlOUWEnSxdj-hzgHEdw5RQgrhOTwA6q6o?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/6408ada1-f04e-4ee1-9224-00f658e90b01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
This article went out this morning, but I scheduled it to go out last night. It's possible that, by the time this gets sent, the House will have already passed the bill.
Also, it needs to be said: what an embarrassingly stupid name for a pretty consequential piece of legislation.
2
There are also important differences between debt and deficit that I don't really get into, because they're complicated and don't impact my broader point. With a little help from our friends at ChatGPT, [here's a quick (and oversimplified) explainer of the difference](https://substack.com/redirect/50447cdb-4aec-4d1c-a8e7-5923c723930b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) between the two.
3
I generally respect Lisa Murkowski, but this wasn't her finest moment. She's [telling reporters](https://substack.com/redirect/60fd640e-ca98-41b6-bb4c-2db1f5c0ba5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that her vote was an "agonizing" choice, but a tortured yes vote is still a yes vote.
4
The Republican Party, [in their own platform](https://substack.com/redirect/fff6abb7-3d5f-4598-bc14-39876583a4e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), talks about the need to "improve fiscal sanity" and "expand access to new Affordable Healthcare." This bill, of course, does the opposite.
(Another unimportant note: why are Republicans so damn bad at capitalization?)
5
Not for nothing: a quarter of that [money ends up abroad](https://substack.com/redirect/6c8031d0-8ef7-453a-bb6e-c7a26293537c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), because a lot of our [debt is held by foreign investors](https://substack.com/redirect/f385d3b3-e28d-465d-a864-5933741a0bdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
6
The S&P 500 is at all-time highs despite the threat of tariffs and war in the Middle East and an incredibly unstable U.S. President and increasing isolationism in the world and a million other things. I don't get it. But this is why I don't try to time markets. [Markets can stay irrational longer than you can stay solvent](https://substack.com/redirect/eb941be0-ef12-4f52-bd41-3b08fa6d1b67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
7
Sources:
* Mike Johnson, [before](https://substack.com/redirect/977483e2-be69-4872-9e8c-f377ac337699?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) vs. [after](https://substack.com/redirect/9b3c08fd-b037-4e5f-9fe5-5235955c4f8d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Jason Smith, [before](https://substack.com/redirect/b033aa05-6929-4d57-8a1f-f48b89457fa0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) vs. [after](https://substack.com/redirect/288831c0-48e7-4659-bcf3-edc9144d419d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Tom Emmer, [before](https://substack.com/redirect/8736c4c0-a2fd-4b28-a29e-2a1a0d5c445c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) vs. [after](https://substack.com/redirect/8a96a1ee-5a38-4cf6-9f0e-4dce8f0cab0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Jodey Arrington, [before](https://substack.com/redirect/d2d229fc-1a07-47cc-b7ba-be5bf86b9dbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) vs. [after](https://substack.com/redirect/d2d229fc-1a07-47cc-b7ba-be5bf86b9dbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* John Thune, [before](https://substack.com/redirect/7da786e4-3566-4960-970c-68341d2d0e25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (His "after" is obvious; it's just everything he's done over the last few days.)

8
As always, _xkcd_ has some wonderful observations, in this case on [how to properly contextualize large numbers](https://substack.com/redirect/cff9f502-ab91-48b6-bc31-fbf4bf3a3022?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). ([Here's another](https://substack.com/redirect/d3f127af-2b75-46de-873d-318977f9653c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) great, though less fun, _xkcd_ on the same topic.)
9
This is especially true given our [declining birth rates](https://substack.com/redirect/c34c7be4-4ed9-4c9e-8ff1-2501fcb1de46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Trump's intense aversion to immigration. If our population isn't growing, which it likely won't if those trends continue, it'll exacerbate these risks even more.
10

Over time, that will take money away from the things that the federal government can--and should--be spending money on. 3. Democrats have a unique opportunity to position themselves as the party of responsive, responsible, and restrained government. ? Article summary: The government already spends nearly $900 billion per year paying interest on our debt. It 's about to go way up, to about $1.6 trillion by 2034. For a party that 's complained for years about America's ballooning federal debt, Republican hypocrisy on this bill is staggering. Younger voters believe that the federal government is wasteful, and young people are very concerned that they 'll never attain financial security. Democrats have a chance to win back young voters, if they can be responsive to that (reasonable) fear. Context on the "Big Beautiful Bill" Before I get into anything related to how much it costs: the policies themselves are really bad. A few examples: Nonpartisan government estimates suggest that because of Medicaid cuts. Cuts to food stamps () will impact many of the who rely on the program. It drastically cuts support for clean energy in a way that , to say nothing of the impact it'll have on the climate. For a bill that's set to extend tax cuts for many Americans, it's : 54% of Americans believe it 'll have a "mostly negative" impact on the country, and only 30% say its impact will be "mostly positive."4 Context on how much our debt costs us I'm not an economist, and I don't have any strong opinion on what the "right" levels of debt are for the country--lots of much smarter, much better educated people than I disagree on that question. But as a statement of fact: the more the national debt grows, the more taxpayers have to spend on interest payments. And we're about to add , if not more. In 2024--riding a wave of spending and borrowing coming out of the pandemic that hasn't abated--the American taxpayer spends more to pay down our debt than we do on Medicare.5 | | ---|---|--- This bill takes things even further: it'll increase the federal government's annual interest payments from $870 billion to . It's almost impossible to contextualize how large that number is, so to give you some sense: The cost of . The total cost of all education in the U.S.--preschool through graduate school, public and private--. Those are both less than how much we'll be paying in interest every year. People in Congress are constantly talking about the debt and the deficit, so it 's important to note that this isn't normal. For a century, debt levels have never been at 0%--there are the more money that we borrow, the more money we have to spend paying off what we borrow. In that sense, it's not much different than a mortgage. When borrowing is lower during more stable periods, it gives the country more flexibility to spend and borrow during moments of true crisis. It made some sense to borrow money when the world briefly shut down during the COVID-19 pandemic, because that helped keep people fed and housed. But right now, our debt-to-GDP ratio is , which is really concerning because we're hardly in a moment of crisis: The stock market is having rebounded from the carnage earlier this year. Unemployment . Inflation has , midway through the Biden Presidency. of the Trump presidency, but only very slightly. It grew meaningfully from the pandemic through the end of 2024. What does this mean? When a true crisis comes again--and it will, sooner or later--we're hamstringing our ability to spend money to curtail a deeper crisis, or to fund a war. Increasing the deficit for me, not for thee Complaining about the other party's profligate, irresponsible spending is a time-tested Washington tradition. But for a party that (nominally) cares about responsible spending, Republicans are awfully silent when they're in power. Here's a side-by-side comparison of what Republicans were saying during debates around Biden's vs. what they're saying now when it comes to spending and/or the federal debt.7 | | ---|---|--- This doesn't get talked about enough: the Congressional Budget Office said that Biden's Inflation Reduction Act . Conservative think tanks , but you know what they weren't saying? That it was . Again, numbers that large are hard to contextualize (or at least they are for me),8 and--it's a nuanced but important distinction--debt and deficit are not the same thing. But: the fact that we are taking on so much debt, and the fact that there's seemingly no Republican consideration for the long-term costs of what we're doing, is really bad. They're not fooling the American public, by the way. In a recent poll, that this will increase the budget deficit than believe it'll reduce it. Democrats have an opportunity to win back young voters I'm not an especially cynical person, or at least I try not to be. But I've been paying into Social Security for as long as I've been working, and I've long assumed that I'll never see any benefit.9 And I'm not alone in this: 39% of millennials (and 45% of Gen Z) up by the time that we retire. Nearly half of people under 30 . Anecdotally, I've felt--and Gen Z is an intensely cynical generation, in stark contrast to my more optimistic millennial peers. So it's notable that of Trump's signature ideas and policies, is the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE): | | ---|---|--- I've written before about how in the '24 election. But a dynamic that I haven't talked enough about: Perhaps their support for a better, more efficient government helps explain part of that trend. , but young people are right to believe . The monstrosity of a bill that's likely to land on Trump's desk soon creates an opportunity for Democrats to own the issue of government efficiency. Republicans are spending with no regard for the future, and young voters--both men and women--understand that they're the ones who'll have to pay for it. People want government to work better for them. But Republicans don't seem to care. And while this isn't the only way to win back young voters, demonstrating that Democrats actually care about our future --and that they're not just borrowing against it--is a start. ? name: Mark Kelly
subject: There will be consequences
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
The choice in yesterday’s vote in the Senate was clear: support working families or give handouts to billionaires. Trump and Congressional Republicans chose the billionaires.
#

Team,
The choice in yesterday’s vote in the Senate was clear: support working families or give handouts to billionaires. Trump and Congressional Republicans chose the billionaires.
While families across Arizona are trying to afford groceries or take their kids to the doctor, this bill gives another tax break to the ultra-wealthy so they can buy another private jet.
We offered amendment after amendment to make this bill better: making rich people pay their fair share while giving tax cuts to the middle class, keeping health care coverage and food assistance for working people, and protecting clean energy jobs. Republicans rejected every single one of them.
I hoped more of my Republican colleagues would have the guts to stand up to Donald Trump, and now, they’ll have to explain it to the people they represent.
What I can tell you is that this is not going away. They voted to take health care from 17 million people, and I’m going to make sure they face the consequences.
[**I will be fighting tooth and nail to help retake the House and Senate next year so we can roll back the damage Trump and his loyalists have done. Please, chip in a grassroots donation to help.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-july25/.CXgdOY&refcode=em250702-1-600&t=1&akid=6112%2E6688874%2EdCKSEn)

While families across Arizona are trying to afford groceries or take their kids to the doctor, this bill gives another tax break to the ultra-wealthy so they can buy another private jet. We offered amendment after amendment to make this bill better: making rich people pay their fair share while giving tax cuts to the middle class, keeping health care coverage and food assistance for working people, and protecting clean energy jobs. Republicans rejected every single one of them. I hoped more of my Republican colleagues would have the guts to stand up to Donald Trump, and now, they’ll have to explain it to the people they represent. What I can tell you is that this is not going away. They voted to take health care from 17 million people, and I’m going to make sure they face the consequences. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, Mark Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate

Then, they came for our Social Security and Medicare.

So Hakeem Jeffries just STOOD UP and announced a massive 10-point plan to TAKE ON Trump and Republicans!

name: SAF 2025 Census
subject: Participation encouraged for the 2025 Census
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Second Amendment Foundation
body:
Our team wants to review yours as soon as possible

---
| | | | | | | Untitled Document [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zod50303sn6jkoknf95jq5p4tqkjrd841ef512kg_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)

Friend,

You have been _specifically selected_ to complete the **[Official 2025 Census,](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zl01echr4gm3ms7ehdsoaqkrsfqn83s7glatf4bo_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)** and not by chance. Out of hundreds of patriots in **your city,** our team chose YOU because **your voice matters in this fight.**
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zqr1u35bvde4veq3hujrjvjtap74rhcgrmcgt2go_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)
This year brings real threats to the Second Amendment: AR-15 bans, age-based gun purchase restrictions, and even a **national gun registry.** _But with enough pressure, 2025 could also be the year we push back harder than ever and defeat the gun-grabbers once and for all._

The Second Amendment Foundation has already had massive wins, **but major cases are pending.** Your input is required if we are going to MAKE A DIFFERENCE and FORTIFY our gun rights.

Our team wants to review every **[2025 Census](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z8nktnhqs0faln7rm4vl293d11lgtqmp1hkr7dug_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)** response **tonight** to help shape our strategy, and we need yours before midnight. This is your chance to directly influence the direction of the pro-gun movement in 2025.

_**[Please complete the Official 2025 Census before the mandated deadline of MIDNIGHT TONIGHT to bolster the Second Amendment movement.>>](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zu4ehacdl50bml4eqr6st374cmbttt7lbhaakuh0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)**_
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z5eg0ldhqgnk1p655e5gl4uobcqkpdhrmfrrl1so_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs) The 2024 Census helped us sharpen our grassroots efforts, fight gun control bills in hostile states, and win critical legal battles. The 2025 Census will be even more important, **and we can't afford a single missing response.**

**We 're counting on you, Friend. Don't sit this one out.** The clock's ticking. Midnight is the deadline.

_**[Follow your secure link here to complete the Official 2025 Census before the deadline.>>](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zqcak25oqf2qoc8i0ecv14kenhk73e4ikjqt78mo_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)**_

Thank you for standing with us,

SAF Census Team
****[TAKE CENSUS NOW>>](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z0vmpi1moe9d0cj8rlefrlbolh8p4ptr4b9t40fo_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)****

|
---
| Paid for by Second Amendment Foundation.|

Pop Acta Media | Your America
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z6beokmtvcg7rojbobpsk0gfohste4t1h6nbq3s0_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkjrrasjk6mqj4t5m6gpq7d9neprqastmelp3cthieip3adt7skqu2c3neior3d5medjm05plmgqb3ct66uqr695lshgm6pb1clh648d245p25oh4ctim8ob7ctgm8o324no)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z6ogs6v3ckuop33l76p8nsfaiuqrsip813cko7co_rp22sh2s8i6epb4c5jmeob4c1h2bs)_

[](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zcm7iamh5bhk0j8ovtv3abiak80n2j1a4dakhni8_rp2ash2s8j2ccdkmorb4dthe8gq48bi4bh28pr5chgmepr1chg649fg)

body:
We need to know if you still watch CBS...
| Donald Trump is going after PBS, NPR, MSNBC, __and now CBS!__ [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=6&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8) | [**EMERGENCY FLASH POLL: Do you still watch CBS? »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=7&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8) | [YES, I DO! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=8&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
---
[NO, I DON'T »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=9&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
**** Please respond within 10 minutes of opening this email to ensure your response is counted ****
**Trump can’t take ANY criticism, so he’s going after _any_ media outlet that tells the truth about his authoritarian and unconstitutional antics.**

The good news: _Bernie Sanders is fighting back!_
**“In the USA, presidents do not punish or censor the media for criticizing them. Freedom of the press is what sets us apart from tin pot dictatorships and authoritarian regimes.” - Senator Bernie Sanders**
---
CBS is under threat, and we need Democrats EVERYWHERE to join Bernie Sanders in the fight to protect it. _That’s why we need to know:_

[**Friend, do you watch CBS? »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=10&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
[YES, I DO! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=11&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
---
[NO, I DON'T »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/cbs_viewership_poll/?source=VPP_20250702_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=12&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
**** Please respond within 10 minutes of opening this email to ensure your response is counted ****
Thank you for joining our fight,

Team Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=13&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20451%2E878179%2EeLxER8)
---

clean_body: Peter, Things are unraveling fast on the Left. One top Democrat insider just came clean and said the quiet part out loud: Yikes. But don't let that fool you into thinking we can sit back. When the Democrats start to panic, they throw every wild idea they've got at the wall just to see what sticks. Meanwhile, over here? and YOUR name is STILL missing from the list. have already contacted you to sound the alarm--and they are wondering why you haven't checked in yet. This isn't just a formality. It's how we make sure our team is ready to show up, stand strong and win. Let's finish what we started -- and show the Left we mean business. - Senate Republican Voter Verification Committee | Paid for by the NRSC. When Madison Horn ran for Congress in Oklahoma last year, she noticed that her local news station regularly featured her Republican opponent on their broadcasts while ignoring her requests to be a guest on their shows.

**Now that we know the programs we’ve designed are WORKING, we’re doubling down on our support – and gearing up for November. Here is a look at a few of our endorsed candidates who won their primaries and will be on the ballot this November:**

✅ **Erica Deuso for Mayor of Downingtown, Pennsylvania.**
After winning a tough primary against a Democratic opponent who proudly called himself a “white nationalist”... Erica will be on the November ballot against Richard Bryant, a far-right extremist and unapologetic Trump supporter. He is known to mock women for their appearance, attack the LGBTQ+ community, and spread harmful lies about climate change and immigrants. Erica is a well-respected scientist, businesswoman, and advocate for marginalized communities. **Her primary victory brings Downingtown one step closer to electing Pennsylvania’s first openly trans Mayor (!!!!), but she needs our continued help to win in November!**

✅ **Senator Ghazala Hashmi for Lieutenant Governor of Virginia.** It was our honor to support Senator Hashmi in her statewide race for this office. **_Our team spoke to voters all over the state of VA on her behalf_** and had some really productive conversations.

Hashmi was the only woman running against _six_ _male_ _opponents_ in this primary. She was also the most experienced legislator in her race. As State Senator, Hashmi helped draft Virginia’s constitutional amendment protecting safe and legal abortion.

This November, Hashmi will face off against a Republican talk-radio host, John Reid. His campaign website features dangerous disinformation like, [“John Reid is pro life and has boldly spoken out on TV, radio, and in his personal testimony against the Democrats new radical unlimited any time-any place, day of birth abortion proposals.”]

✅ **May Nivar for Virginia House of Delegates.** May is running in the most flippable district in Virginia – the same seat Susanna Gibson ran for in 2023 and lost by only _715_ votes after enduring a sexist and horrifying attack from the state’s Republican party.

This November, May is working to unseat her GOP Representative, who was found to be the **least effective legislator** in Virginia. He has said he would like to see abortion become illegal in Virginia and has repeatedly voted against common sense gun safety policies, paid medical leave, bills to raise the minimum wage, lower prescription drug costs, and more... We need to ensure May wins her General election.

[**There are so many more candidates on our roster who we are incredibly proud to see moving on to general elections across the U.S. We are ready to continue our important – and effective – work. Help us keep up our momentum – and keep our programs running by donating $20.25 or more today!**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hbm-pac/?refcode=EM_F_250701doubtC_N_JZ_NO_9_NG_N_L4&amount=20.25)

Together,

Jordan Zaslow
Founder & President
Her Bold Move

Her primary victory brings Downingtown one step closer to electing Pennsylvania’s first openly trans Mayor (!!!!), but she needs our continued help to win in November! ✅ Senator Ghazala Hashmi for Lieutenant Governor of Virginia. It was our honor to support Senator Hashmi in her statewide race for this office. Our team spoke to voters all over the state of VA on her behalf and had some really productive conversations. Hashmi was the only woman running against six male opponents in this primary. She was also the most experienced legislator in her race. As State Senator, Hashmi helped draft Virginia’s constitutional amendment protecting safe and legal abortion. This November, Hashmi will face off against a Republican talk-radio host, John Reid. His campaign website features dangerous disinformation like, [“John Reid is pro life and has boldly spoken out on TV, radio, and in his personal testimony against the Democrats new radical unlimited any time-any place, day of birth abortion proposals.”] ✅ May Nivar for Virginia House of Delegates. May is running in the most flippable district in Virginia – the same seat Susanna Gibson ran for in 2023 and lost by only 715 votes after enduring a sexist and horrifying attack from the state’s Republican party. This November, May is working to unseat her GOP Representative, who was found to be the least effective legislator in Virginia. He has said he would like to see abortion become illegal in Virginia and has repeatedly voted against common sense gun safety policies, paid medical leave, bills to raise the minimum wage, lower prescription drug costs, and more... We need to ensure May wins her General election. Together, Jordan Zaslow Founder & President Her Bold Move | | Her Bold Move is the fastest-growing support network for women running for political office in America. As dangerous laws about women get passed in states with the lowest numbers of women in government (11%, 12%....), we're BREAKING the male supermajorities across those states. Because men shouldn't be making laws about women's bodies. Support this important work with a contribution today!** = | | | | | Paid for by Her Bold Move Action. Her Bold Move One Park Row 5th Floor Providence, RI 02903 United States This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. GOP Rep. Brandon Gill **[fired back at Democrat loudmouth Dan Goldman](https://substack.com/redirect/4310fed7-7c4a-4a02-97d5-f7db829854f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for having the audacity to accuse Republicans and Trump of lawfare after the Democrats tried to put their political enemies behind bars in 2024.
Gill goes scorched earth...
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/4310fed7-7c4a-4a02-97d5-f7db829854f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4310fed7-7c4a-4a02-97d5-f7db829854f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"This is the accusations from my colleagues on the other side of the aisle!" Goldman cried. "That the search of Mar-a-Lago for classified documents after Donald Trump repeatedly obstructed justice... that that search warrant is suddenly 'unprecedented lawfare' and must be politically motivated!'"
**["Lawfare is antithetical to the American experiment!"](https://substack.com/redirect/4310fed7-7c4a-4a02-97d5-f7db829854f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Gill fired back at Goldman. "Yet it seems to have been the Democrat's last ditch effort to win the 2024 election cycle... they knew they couldn't fairly win!"
"Their last hope leading up to the election was to concoct a novel legal theory by Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg... they finally got their wish, they landed a conviction of the president. The American people knew it was a SHAM and now they know the lengths the other side of the aisle will go to attack their political opposition!"
Watch the Texas congressman go to battle against the Dems:
[Click To Watch](https://substack.com/redirect/4310fed7-7c4a-4a02-97d5-f7db829854f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **[Deep State Fires A Warning Shot At Your Vote And Your Future](https://substack.com/redirect/7c921ef8-eae3-41de-b14d-1a802a922fd1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

It wasn't long ago there was an attempt to _**put Trump behind bars**_.
To _**bankrupt him**_ through legal fees.
And to _**even assassinate him**_ ... twice!
But President Trump has prevailed each and every time. And despite having overcome these hits, one after another, the battle may still be far from over.
President Trump has repeatedly stated he wants to _**"demolish the deep state, "**_ and claims this to be his _**" final battle"**_**** against these shadowy figures.
But the truth is... the battle may have only just begun.
Rogue Judges across the country have tried to stop his actions at every turn. Congress hasn't been much help in passing his legislation, and the media is at his throat like always.
So, _**how will Trump bring prosperity to the American People**_ while the Deep State runs rampant, putting up roadblocks for everything he does for America?
_**Will he be able to succeed in Making America Great Again?**_
Only time will tell, but thousands of American Patriots are preparing themselves for a worse case scenario. They _ARE NOT_ leaving things up to chance. They are helping to _**protect themselves**_ in case the Deep State has one more potential _ace up their sleeve._
